# Welcome {.unnumbered}

This is the online version of **The admiral Book**, a guide to creating ADaM
(Analysis Data Model) datasets using the
[`{admiral}`](https://pharmaverse.github.io/admiral/) R package.

## What is admiral?

`{admiral}` (**A**DaM in **R** **A**sset **L**ibrary) is an R package developed
by the [pharmaverse](https://pharmaverse.org/) community. It provides a
modular, open-source toolbox for building ADaM datasets from SDTM data.

The package follows the CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide (ADaMIG) and provides a
rich set of functions for the most common ADaM derivations. Each derivation is
provided as a function call, making it easy to build, audit, and adjust ADaM
programs.

## What is this book for?

This book is a teaching resource that accompanies the `{admiral}` package. It:

-   Explains the concepts and conventions used across all `{admiral}` functions.
-   Walks through end-to-end examples of how to create each major ADaM dataset
    type (ADSL, BDS, OCCDS, etc.).
-   Covers special topics such as date/time imputation, lab grading, and
    estimands.
-   Uses real SDTM data from the
    [`{pharmaversesdtm}`](https://pharmaverse.github.io/pharmaversesdtm/)
    package (based on the CDISC Pilot Study) so that all examples are fully
    reproducible.

## How to use this book

The book is organised into four parts:

1.  **Getting Started** — introduces `{admiral}` and the programming concepts
    and conventions shared across the entire package.
2.  **ADaM Data Structures** — step-by-step guides for creating each major ADaM
    dataset type.
3.  **Special Topics** — in-depth coverage of specific techniques (imputation,
    visits, lab grading, etc.).
4.  **Advanced Topics** — more complex dataset types and frequently asked
    questions.

We recommend first-time readers work through the chapters in order. Experienced
users may prefer to jump directly to the chapter that covers the dataset or
feature they need.

## Prerequisites

To run the code examples in this book you will need R and the following
packages:

```r
install.packages(c(
  "admiral",
  "pharmaversesdtm",
  "dplyr",
  "lubridate",
  "stringr",
  "tibble"
))
```

## Getting help

-   [admiral Slack channel](https://pharmaverse.slack.com/) — for questions and
    discussion.
-   [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/pharmaverse/admiral/issues) — to report
    bugs or request features in the package.
-   [Book Issues](https://github.com/pharmaverse/admiral-book/issues) — to
    report issues with this book.
